currency.name: Libyan dinar (LYD)
currency.note: Currency has parallel rates. Banking system is barely functional. USD preferred.

crypto.note: No infrastructure due to instability. Limited interest. Chaos prevents meaningful adoption.

article:
🧭 **Overview**
Libya is a North African nation with vast oil reserves, Mediterranean coast, and Sahara desert. Muammar Gaddafi ruled 1969-2011 (eccentric dictator). NATO-backed uprising (2011) killed Gaddafi but triggered civil war continuing today. The country is divided: UN-recognized government in Tripoli (west), rival government in Tobruk (east, backed by General Haftar), plus militias, ISIS remnants, and foreign mercenaries (Turkey, Russia/Wagner, UAE). Oil infrastructure is battleground. Tripoli and Benghazi are war zones. For expats, Libya means oil sector hardship postings or humanitarian work in failed state conditions.

👥 **People & vibe**
With roughly 7 million people, Libya is Arab/Berber with tribal divisions. Arabic is language. Islam (Sunni) dominates. Libyan culture is tribal, conservative, and shaped by Gaddafi's 42-year rule and subsequent chaos. The vibe is war-weary survival. Tripoli is militia-controlled; Benghazi is rebuilding under Haftar; south is lawless. Trust is limited to tribe/family. Human trafficking through Libya to Europe is crisis. Kidnapping risk is high.

[[MAP]]

🌦️ **Climate & landscape**
Expect desert climate: scorching summers (35-45°C), mild winters (10-20°C). The landscape is 90% Sahara desert, Mediterranean coast (1,770km), and occasional oases. Ancient Roman ruins (Leptis Magna, Sabratha) are UNESCO sites but damaged by conflict. Natural beauty exists but access is impossible due to security.

🏠 **Housing & settling in**
Tripoli and Benghazi have minimal housing for foreigners — secured compounds only. Expect employer-provided accommodation with armed security. Quality is basic despite costs. Power cuts are frequent. Water is unreliable. Internet is slow. Outside cities, infrastructure doesn't exist. Living is bunker mentality. Movement is restricted and dangerous.

💼 **Work & economy**
The economy is oil-dependent (95% of exports) but production is disrupted by conflict — militias shut down oilfields for leverage. For foreigners, opportunities are limited to oil companies (heavily secured operations), UN agencies, or NGOs. Work permits theoretically required but system is dysfunctional. Salaries include massive hardship premiums. Security risks limit operations. Starting a business is impossible.

🛂 **Visa & entry**
Visa requirements are opaque. Tourist visas don't exist (Libya is not tourist destination). Work visas require employer sponsorship and extensive security clearances. Entry/exit is restricted. Travel between Tripoli and Benghazi is dangerous. Israeli passport holders banned. System is barely functional.

🏥 **Healthcare**
Healthcare is catastrophic. Hospitals are damaged, under-resourced, no medicines, no power. Doctors emigrated. Medical evacuation to Tunisia or Malta is essential for any serious issue. Life expectancy has declined. War injuries, malnutrition, infectious diseases are rampant. International health insurance with evacuation is mandatory.

🚗 **Transport & mobility**
Tripoli has minimal transport — avoid public options. Armored vehicles with security escorts only. Checkpoints everywhere controlled by militias. Roads are damaged. Fuel shortages despite oil wealth. Intercity travel is extremely dangerous — kidnapping, ambushes. Tripoli and Benghazi airports operate sporadically with security threats.

🍛 **Food note (national dish)**
The national dish is **Couscous with meat and vegetables**: North African staple. Alternatively, **Bazeen** (barley dough with meat stew). Libyan cuisine is Maghrebi but food security is issue due to conflict. Street food is risky.

🔎 **Bottom line**
Libya is NOT for general expats. It's exclusively for oil sector personnel on extremely lucrative hardship contracts with comprehensive security, humanitarian workers in dire conditions, or journalists (extreme risk). The country is failed state: two rival governments, militias control territory, ISIS presence, human trafficking, and ongoing conflict. Security situation is catastrophic. Kidnapping is real threat. Infrastructure is destroyed. Only come with institutional support, security protocols, and clear evacuation plan. This is one of world's most dangerous postings.

Expat Score — 2.5 / 10

national_dish.name: Couscous / Bazeen
national_dish.note: Couscous with meat or barley dough with stew — North African Maghrebi cuisine but food security is issue due to conflict.

lgbt_acceptance.note: Homosexuality is illegal. Social attitudes are extremely hostile. Militias execute LGBTQ+ people. Extremely dangerous. No protections whatsoever.

personality.name: Muammar Gaddafi
personality.story: Eccentric dictator (1969-2011) who ruled 42 years. Green Book ideology, bizarre speeches at UN, sponsoring terrorism, then reconciliation with West. Overthrown in 2011 uprising, killed brutally. His death triggered chaos continuing today.

cards.title: 🛢️ Oil wealth, total chaos
cards.text: Libya has Africa's largest oil reserves yet is failed state. Militias shut down oilfields for leverage. Production fluctuates wildly. Wealth doesn't reach citizens. It's resource curse extreme — oil finances militias, not development.

cards.title: 🔫 Two governments + militias
cards.text: UN-recognized Government of National Unity in Tripoli vs House of Representatives in Tobruk (backed by General Haftar) plus hundreds of militias. No monopoly on violence. The state doesn't exist. It's armed groups controlling territory.

cards.title: 🌊 Human trafficking hub
cards.text: Libyan coast is departure point for African migrants trying to reach Europe. Human traffickers run slave markets — literal auctions of humans. Detention centers are torture sites. EU tries to stop boats. The human rights catastrophe is ongoing.

cards.title: 🇷🇺 Wagner + Turkey = proxy war
cards.text: Russia's Wagner mercenaries back Haftar (east). Turkey backs Tripoli government. UAE supports Haftar too. Libya is proxy battlefield. Foreign fighters, weapons, drones pour in. Libyan sovereignty doesn't exist.

cards.title: 🏛️ Roman ruins at risk
cards.text: Leptis Magna, Sabratha — spectacular Roman sites. UNESCO World Heritage. But looting, conflict damage, neglect threaten them. ISIS damaged sites. Cultural heritage is collateral damage in chaos.

cards.title: 💔 Gaddafi's bizarre legacy
cards.text: Green Book ideology, all-female bodyguards, tents at summits, rambling UN speeches — Gaddafi was eccentric dictator. But he kept order. His violent overthrow created vacuum. Libyans debate: brutal stability vs chaotic freedom? Neither is good.
